reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing this for a couple months, and the game has come a long way in that short amount of time. The dev listens to all critiques & updates the game regularly with player suggestions. Ive played a bunch of bow/arrow games on quest and I’d say the mechanics rank up there with the best… not THE best, but very good.  The only issues I’ve come across are quality of life improvements, such as making it easier to know the objective of the hole. I’m confident all the minor issues will be resolved very quickly… this dev listens to EVERYONE. 
Either way, this is a solid pickup, and if you have some friends to play with, it’ll be blast.

Bow Course is a refreshingly original VR game that brilliantly blends golf mechanics with archery. Instead of clubs and balls, you’ll be drawing your bow and letting arrows fly to progress down beautifully designed courses—all with the goal of landing a bullseye in as few shots as possible.

The real twist? You’re not limited to standard arrows. You'll unlock and use a variety of special arrows like the Putter Arrow for precision, the Light Arrow for tricky curves, and the Bouncing Arrow to navigate obstacles. These power-ups bring clever strategy to every shot and keep each course feeling fresh and fun.

To top it all off, the game includes multiplayer, so you can challenge your friends, compare scores, and show off your archery-golf hybrid skills.

Whether you're into target sports, puzzle-style challenges, or just looking for something different in VR—Bow Course is a blast.

Ready, aim, score!

You have a bunch of different arrows to pick from, random wind, shortcuts, various settings and of course adjustable shot strength to make every shot and round unique and giving you a bunch of tactical decisions. You also have a limited number of most arrows and have to use them strategically.

It's quite fun to improve your own highscore or play with friends.

A training mode would be nice where you can freely try out shooting from different positions without having to worry about your limited arrow or attempt amount. Also being able to jump to specific tracks or repeat them would be nice.
